data.title: Blaize, NeoTensr enter into contract for up to $50M to deploy AI edge data center infrastructure
data.description:
• Blaize (BZAI) on Thursday announced an up to $50M contract with hardware and software system integrator NeoTensr to co-develop AI edge data center infrastructure, targeting Asia-Pacific’s growing compute demand.
  • The contract is expected to generate up to $50M in revenue within the first year, the companies said.
  • The offering includes co-branded AI servers and full-stack solutions capable of handling 200+ camera streams per server.
  • Blaize Holdings (BZAI) closed at $1.73, up 4.85%, and extended gains in after-hours trading to $2.13, up 23.12% following the announcement.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DoE rejects CenterPoint Energy's request to close Indiana coal plant
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Energy rejected a request from CenterPoint Energy (CNP) to allow the 60-year-old F.B. Culley coal plant in Indiana to close, Bloomberg reported Thursday, demonstrating the Trump administration's push to keep aging coal plants online that it says are needed to support an electric grid under stress from rising power demand.

CenterPoint (CNP) had asked the DoE not to renew the stay-open mandate for the plant, saying that extending an order halting the planned December 2025 retirement would require millions of dollars in upgrades and lengthy outages to "support an inefficient and increasingly unreliable asset."

The February 17 letter asked the DoE not to renew its December order keeping the plant open, but the agency extended that directive, along with another order for an Indiana coal unit owned by the Northern Indiana Public Service Company, through June 21 in March.

"This letter shows that even utilities like CenterPoint admit that there is no grid emergency and that coal plants are too unreliable, expensive, and polluting to continue operating," Citizens Action Coalition program director Ben Inskeep told Bloomberg.

data.title: Baseball streaming rights see federal antitrust scrutiny - report
data.description:
US regulators are examining the distribution of streaming rights for Major League Baseball as part of a larger federal probe into how professional sports leagues provide their games to online platforms.

Federal Communications Commission Chairman Brendan Carr said that he's listening to baseball fans who are complaining about where to watch MLB games, according to an interview with Bloomberg. 

'One thing that did surprise me was the level of interest of baseball fans in particular,” Carr told Bloomberg.

The probe comes after the WSJ reported last Thursday that the Department of Justice is investigating whether the National Football League’s policy of splitting its broadcast privileges is anticompetitive and harms consumers.

“You could make the argument that there are other sports leagues out there that are potentially pushing the limits of the Sports Broadcasting Act even further than what the NFL has,” Carr said. “The NFL is something that everyone is aware of and focuses on. And so I speak of it just as a shorthand, but we are focused more broadly on other leagues as well.”

The DOJ also intends to examine the streaming rights of other leagues covered under the Sports Broadcasting Act, including MLB, Bloomberg reported, citing a person familiar with the matter. A spokesperson for the DOJ declined to comment.

data.title: Janus Henderson shareholders approve Trian/General Catalyst deal
data.description:
Janus Henderson Group (JHG) shareholders approved the previously announced go-private transaction with Nelson Peltz's Trian Fund Management and General Catalyst Group, the company said Thursday. 

Based on the preliminary voting results, 99.7% of the votes cast, representing 83% of the total outstanding Janus Henderson shares as of March 9, 2026, voted in favor of the transaction.

The acquisition is expected to close in mid-2026.

Under the terms of the deal, Trian and General Catalyst will pay $52.00 per share in cash for each Janus Henderson (JHG) share.

data.title: BNY raises 2026 revenue outlook to ~6% as it expects ~10% net interest income growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: BNY (BK) Q1 2026

Management view

  • CEO Robin Vince said BNY “started the year with a strong performance in the first quarter,” citing “earnings per share of $2.24,” “record revenue of $5.4 billion,” “pre-tax margin expansion to 37%,” and “a return on tangible common equity of 29%.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Robin Vince)
  • Vince highlighted multi-product client wins, including: “Allianz Global Investors… has selected BNY,” “PayPal has selected BNY to provide institutional-grade digital asset custody,” and “the U.S. Treasury Department announced that they have selected BNY as financial agent for Trump accounts… and collaborate with Robinhood.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince)
  • Vince framed AI as a key strategic enabler, saying “we made Eliza available to 100% of our employees,” “develop more than 200 AI solutions,” and in 2026 “doubling down on depth, moving from AI point solutions to using AI to enhance end-to-end processes.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince)
  • CFO Dermot McDonogh attributed revenue strength to activity and mix, stating, “Total revenue of $5.4 billion was up 13% year-over-year,” and “foreign exchange revenue was up 49% year-over-year on the back of higher volumes.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO Dermot McDonogh)

Outlook

  • McDonogh raised the full-year top line view: “we are raising our outlook for total revenue, excluding notable items for the full year 2026 and now expect approximately 6% year-over-year growth.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • McDonogh updated net interest income expectations: “That includes our expectation for full year 2026 net interest income to be up approximately 10% year-over-year.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • McDonogh kept the expense growth range but shifted to the high end: “We expect full year 2026 expense growth, excluding notable items, to be at the top of the 3% to 4% year-over-year growth rate range that we provided in January.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • McDonogh reiterated taxes: “we continue to expect a quarterly tax rate of approximately 23% for the remaining quarters this year.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)

Financial results

  • McDonogh reported fee and balance metrics, including “fee revenue was up 11%,” “firm-wide AUC/A of $59.4 trillion increased by 12% year-over-year,” and “assets under management of $2.1 trillion were up 6%.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • McDonogh detailed revenue composition items: “Investment and other revenue was $271 million… including approximately $135 million of investment-related gains and $50 million of net securities losses.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • McDonogh discussed profitability and expenses, stating “expenses of $3.4 billion were up 5% year-over-year,” “pre-tax margin expanded to 37%,” and “return on tangible common equity was 29%.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • McDonogh highlighted capital actions: “we returned $1.4 billion of capital to our shareholders… and our Board of Directors authorized a new $10 billion share repurchase program.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)

Q&A

  • Brennan Hawken, BMO Capital Markets: asked about deposit trends and euro/sterling betas; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said elevated liquidity drove balances and mix, euro/sterling are “roughly 25% of the overall book,” and “the betas roughly peaked at 80%.”
  • Brennan Hawken, BMO Capital Markets: asked why Wealth Solutions revenue lagged activity; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh pointed to “more of a volume-driven quarter,” reaffirmed “net new assets mid-single-digit growth,” and said Archer integration should “drive more capabilities and more product innovation.”
  • Alexander Blostein, Goldman Sachs: asked how much volatility drove fees/NII baseline; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh cited revenue diversity with “roughly 50-50 between balances and volumes” and noted “a few one-offs… in Securities Services.”
  • Alexander Blostein, Goldman Sachs: asked about deposit reversals in NII guide; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said balances should “revert to more seasonal patterns,” with Q2 “slightly down,” Q3 “weakest,” and scenarios supporting the “10% guide.”
  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA Securities: asked what fee assumptions underpin guidance; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said the company was “not really changing our outlook on the fee at the moment” given uncertainty, while pointing to record sales and “grinding organic growth higher.”
  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA Securities: asked whether BNY is underinvesting given AI-driven productivity; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said “it’s very important to do both” investment and operating leverage, cited “$4 billion technology spend,” “218 AI solutions in production,” and said “if we felt we needed to do more, we could do more.”
  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo: asked for financial benefits of AI over time; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said outcomes should show in “revenue per employee,” product capabilities, and doing work “that… didn’t make a lot of sense to do before.”
  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo: asked about AI-driven cyber risk; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said “Cyber defense” is a priority, referenced “Mythos in-house,” and described AI as a “superpower… used for good… or… evil.”
  • Manan Gosalia, Morgan Stanley: asked why medium-term targets remain 38% +/- despite AI; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said targets set in January are “a stretch,” “too early to say,” and investment could rise; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said AI value will accrue to “clients… employees… and… shareholders,” though some will be “priced out.”
  • Manan Gosalia, Morgan Stanley: asked about new rules and capital targets; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said the rule is “broadly favorable,” shifting from prior “up 5% to 7%” to “a flat to modest reduction.”
  • Kenneth Usdin, Bernstein Autonomous: asked if quarter-end balance spikes change capital return; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said “That’s correct,” noting it was “spot balance sheet… returned to normal levels on April 1.”
  • Kenneth Usdin, Bernstein Autonomous: asked about organic growth amid uncertainty; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh cited “record sales quarter” and said organic growth should “grind higher.”
  • Glenn Schorr, Evercore ISI: asked about exposure to private credit/NBFI lending; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said exposure is “de minimis,” and pointed to Corporate Trust, noting it surpassed “$15 trillion” of loans serviced.
  • Glenn Schorr, Evercore ISI: asked about tokenization rationale and business impact; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said “I don’t think the world needs everything tokenized,” but infrastructure is moving to “more of an always-on operating model,” and BNY aims “to be a bridge.”
  • David Smith, Truist: asked for metrics on multi-product penetration; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince cited “about 10% of our sales with… new logos,” “more than 60% growth… in the number of clients buying from three or more businesses,” and “20% annual increase in sales productivity.”
  • David Smith, Truist: asked if diversification dampens upside in strong markets; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said “we give up some growth” in an “equity markets… up 50%” scenario, adding, “I could tell you to buy somebody else’s stock over ours.”
  • Steven Chubak, Wolfe Research: asked if AI prompts RIAs to self-clear; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said a large client “reaffirm[ed] how excited they were to be on our platform,” citing clients’ desire not to fund “cyber defense” and platform investment.
  • Steven Chubak, Wolfe Research: asked tokenization implications for ADRs; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said DR decline has been predicted “for 20 years,” but it is “a… growing business,” emphasizing infrastructure “connectivity” and trust.
  • Gerard Cassidy, RBC: asked about sequential decline in Issuer Services/DR; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh said DR is “a seasonal business,” while Corporate Trust has “great momentum.”
  • Gerard Cassidy, RBC: asked when AI becomes ubiquitous; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince said it must be “a lot less than those time frames,” warning that being behind a “10x curve” would impair competitiveness.

Sentiment analysis

  • Analyst tone was slightly skeptical on sustainability and modeling, pressing on volatility-driven performance, deposits, fee-growth assumptions, and tangible AI economics, including “how much of the benefit” from volatility and “what will the financial benefits be?” (Alexander Blostein, Goldman Sachs; Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo)
  • Management tone was positive and confident in prepared remarks, and more explanatory in Q&A, repeatedly emphasizing durability and discipline, including “we’re confident that AI will enable us to evolve our business model” and “we have scaled platforms that we’ve invested in.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • Versus last quarter’s emphasis on “increasing our pretax margin target… to 38%” and launching tokenized deposits, the current call leaned more heavily into AI execution metrics and raised 2026 revenue/NII outlook. (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince; Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Guidance shifted upward, with McDonogh moving from January’s “approximately 5% year-over-year” revenue growth view to “approximately 6%,” and framing 2026 NII specifically as “up approximately 10% year-over-year.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • Strategy messaging evolved from Q4’s “next phase” and medium-term target increases (38% pretax margin; 28% ROTCE) toward Q1’s detailed AI scaling narrative, including “more than 200 AI solutions” and “digital employees.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince)
  • Capital deployment added a new authorization in Q1: “a new $10 billion share repurchase program,” while Q4 emphasized capital philosophy and running Tier 1 leverage in the “6% ZIP code.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)

Risks and concerns

  • McDonogh flagged “deposit margin compression” as an offset to NII drivers and said deposit balances are expected to “revert to more seasonal patterns.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)
  • Vince addressed cyber risk from AI-enabled bad actors, calling cyber defense “a team sport” and noting the need to “stay ahead of the curve.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Vince)
  • McDonogh noted CET1 volatility from quarter-end activity, saying the sequential decline was “primarily driven by higher risk-weighted assets” tied to a “single day increase in overnight loan balances.” (Senior Executive VP & CFO McDonogh)

Final takeaway

BNY management described Q1 as a record-revenue quarter supported by broad-based fee growth, higher market activity (notably FX volumes), and operating leverage, alongside a raised 2026 outlook of approximately 6% revenue growth and approximately 10% net interest income growth; leadership also emphasized AI as a core enabler—citing enterprise-wide deployment, a growing set of production solutions, and “digital employees”—while reiterating financial discipline, active capital return (including a new $10 billion repurchase program), and focus on managing deposit seasonality, margin compression, and evolving cyber threats.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: TON Strategy names Kevin Wilson CEO to boost Toncoin treasury returns
data.description:
• TON Strategy (TONX) on Thursday announced Kevin Wilson as CEO, effective May 4, 2026, signaling a leadership shift to scale its Toncoin-focused treasury strategy.
  • Kevin Wilson brings 20+ years of fintech and institutional trading experience, strengthening execution in crypto markets.
  • The company aims to grow $TON per share, boost staking-driven treasury returns, and reduce the gap between the company's book value and market price.
  • The stock price is trading at $2.60, up 2.77%.
  • Source: Press Release
